name: Garlic Steak Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 3 large Green peppers, cut into half-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat against the grain into thin strips (easier if the meat is partially frozen).
2. Make a marinade by combining soy sauce, beef bouillon, ginger, mashed garlic, and sherry. Marinate the steak for 2-12 hours in the refrigerator.
3. Dry the meat on paper towels.
4. In a wok, heat peanut oil over medium-high heat. Add chopped garlic and sauté until golden brown. Remove from the wok, leaving at least 2 tablespoons of oil.
5. Add the meat to the hot oil and sauté until brown. If desired, add just a dash of sherry or sesame oil while browning the meat.
6. Pour in the reserved marinade and 1 cup of boiling water. Simmer for 45 minutes or less time until the meat is tender.
7. Remove the meat and keep it warm. Return the wok to heat and pour the remaining marinade into a separate pan.
8. Add cornstarch, and if needed, add an additional cup of boiling water. Simmer until thickened.
9. Stir-fry green peppers and sliced water chestnuts in the liquid remaining in the wok.
10. Return the meat and marinade gravy to the wok. Add a dash of sesame oil for flavor.
11. Serve over hot boiled rice.
